## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On May 8, 1945, the massive, incredibly joyous, and historically monumental "V-E Day" (Victory in Europe Day) was officially and aggressively celebrated across the globe. Following the absolute, unconditional surrender of Nazi Germany, massive, euphoric crowds aggressively filled the streets of London, Paris, and New York. The massive celebrations deeply marked the absolute, definitive end of the horrific, massive European theatre of World War II, though the incredibly brutal conflict in the Pacific Theater against Imperial Japan continued aggressively.

On May 8, 1886, Dr. John Pemberton, an American pharmacist, officially and aggressively served the very first massive glass of his newly invented carbonated beverage at Jacobs' Pharmacy in Atlanta, Georgia. Originally heavily marketed as a massive, patent medicine and nerve tonic containing trace amounts of cocaine, the deeply popular syrup was heavily formulated into the iconic beverage known absolutely as Coca-Cola, fundamentally launching the most massive, globally recognized consumer brand in human history.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On May 8, 1933, Mahatma Gandhi aggressively initiated a massive, highly publicized 21-day fast while heavily imprisoned by the British colonial administration in Yerwada Jail. This massive, deeply profound hunger strike was specifically and aggressively undertaken to heavily protest the horrific, deeply entrenched Hindu practice of untouchability and to aggressively campaign for the massive social and religious upliftment of the Dalit community, whom he deeply and controversially termed "Harijans" (Children of God).

On May 8, 1916, Swami Chinmayananda Saraswati, the massive, highly revered Hindu spiritual leader and masterful teacher of Advaita Vedanta, was born in Kerala. A brilliant former journalist who aggressively participated in the Indian independence movement, he experienced a massive, profound spiritual awakening. He heavily founded the massive Chinmaya Mission, aggressively dedicating his entire life to the massive, global dissemination of complex Vedic philosophy translated into highly accessible, modern English discourse.
